sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']
before_by_id = {n['id']: n for n in before}
after_by_id = {n['id']: n for n in after}

for nid, n in after_by_id.items():
    if nid in before_by_id:
        bn = before_by_id[nid]
        if n['pixels'] != bn['pixels']:
            print(f"Node {nid}: color={n['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{n['pixels']}")

# Focus on yellow bar (node 16)
for n in after:
    if n['id'] == 16:
        print(f"Yellow bar: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The yellow bar (node 16) shrank from 23 to 14 pixels after clicking on the top Y shape (node 20). So the top Y shape in the container shrinks the yellow bar. By the pattern established with the N shapes, the bottom Y shape (node 23) should grow it back.
Now I understand the mechanic:
- Each container has two shapes: one shrinks and one grows the matching colored object
- For N shapes: left (node 13) shrinks, right (node 14) grows
- For Y shapes: top (node 20) shrinks, bottom (node 23) grows
The goal is to shrink all colored objects completely. I need to:
1. Keep clicking the shrink shapes until objects disappear
2. The green container went from 14→5 with one click, the yellow bar went from 23→14 with one click
Let me keep clicking the shrink shapes. I need to click the green shrink shape multiple times and the yellow shrink shape multiple times until they're fully gone.
Let me batch several clicks on the shrink shapes.

# Yellow bar: 23→14 (need more clicks to fully shrink)
